summary: A light meal can stay light when fullness is met, but meaningful protein gaps should still route back when practical repair room exists.
plain_english: Do not block a breakfast just because calories are light when the fullness factor is met. Calories are easier to make up with finishers. Still push protein into the recipe when the protein gap is meaningful and there is room to repair it.
source_refs: Luke correction 2026-06-04: I do not want to make a habit of all light allowed if there is room for fullness.
Luke correction 2026-06-04: light allowed is ok if the full factor is met; mainly try for protein because calories are easier to make up with finishers.

block.message: Recipe has a meaningful protein gap and still has practical room to improve the portion. Repair the protein or save an explicit light-meal exception.
